req.title: Account-level transaction limits (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) valid limit configuration within regulatory caps (positive/EP), (2) limit exceeding the regulatory cap returning 422 (negative/NEG), (3) boundary value exactly at the regulatory cap (boundary/BVA), (4) cool-down state transition where a second update before 2 hours is rejected or queued (state/STC), and (5) a decision-table case combining channel type and authorization (DT). Five tests are generated to satisfy the minimum of three and provide thorough coverage of the stated error pathways and state model.

tests.name: test_set_valid_daily_limit_within_cap
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested limit is within the regulatory cap (e.g. 4000 < 5000 cap)
actions: Assert response body contains status=pending and effective_at is ~2 hours in the future
expected: Response JSON effective_at is approximately now + 2 hours (within 60-second tolerance)
GET /accounts/{id}/limits returns the pending record with the same values
trace: R-FIN-067: customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps
R-FIN-067: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest, time
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_set_valid_daily_limit_within_cap(api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id):
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 4000}
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    effective_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_at"])
    now_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    expected_effective = now_utc + timedelta(hours=2)
    delta = abs((effective_at - expected_effective).total_seconds())
    assert delta < 60, f"effective_at out of range: {effective_at}"
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pending = next(
        (r for r in get_resp.json()["limits"]
         if r["channel"] == "online" and r["status"] == "pending"),
        None,
    )
    assert pending is not None
    assert pending["daily_limit"] == 4000

tests.name: test_set_limit_exceeding_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory cap for the 'online' channel is known (e.g. 5000)
actions: Assert response body contains a machine-readable error code referencing the cap breach
expected: No pending limit record created for this channel
trace: R-FIN-067: Error pathways — 422 when over regulatory cap
R-FIN-067: Negative paths must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_set_limit_exceeding_regulatory_cap_returns_422(
    api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, regulatory_cap
):
    over_cap = regulatory_cap + 1
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": over_cap},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "LIMIT_EXCEEDS_REGULATORY_CAP"
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pending_over = [
        r for r in get_resp.json()["limits"]
        if r["channel"] == "online"
        and r["status"] == "pending"
        and r["daily_limit"] == over_cap
    ]
    assert pending_over == [], "No pending record should exist for an over-cap limit"

tests.name: test_set_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory cap for 'online' channel is known (e.g. 5000)
expected: Limit value in the pending record equals 5000 (the cap itself)
trace: R-FIN-067: customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps
BVA upper boundary: value == cap must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_set_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted(
    api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, regulatory_cap
):
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": regulatory_cap},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body["daily_limit"] == regulatory_cap
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pending = next(
        (r for r in get_resp.json()["limits"]
         if r["channel"] == "online"
         and r["status"] == "pending"
         and r["daily_limit"] == regulatory_cap),
        None,
    )
    assert pending is not None, "Pending record at exact cap must exist"

tests.name: test_second_limit_update_during_cooldown_is_queued_or_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
A pending limit change already exists for the 'online' channel (submitted < 2 hours ago)
Cool-down period has NOT yet elapsed
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 3000} (first change, seeds pending state)
Assert the second call returns either HTTP 409 Conflict or HTTP 202 with a later effective_at (system-defined behaviour)
expected: Second PATCH returns HTTP 409 Conflict OR HTTP 202 with effective_at >= first effective_at + 2 hours
The active (non-pending) limit is not changed until after cool-down
System does not allow two simultaneous pending changes to collapse into one without tracking
trace: R-FIN-067: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
STC: transition from pending state on receipt of a second update event
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_second_limit_update_during_cooldown_is_queued_or_rejected(
    api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id
):
    first = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 3000},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert first.status_code == 202, first.text
    first_effective = datetime.fromisoformat(first.json()["effective_at"])

    second = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 2500},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert second.status_code in (202, 409), second.text
    if second.status_code == 202:
        second_effective = datetime.fromisoformat(second.json()["effective_at"])
        assert second_effective >= first_effective, (
            "Second pending change must not be effective before first"
        )
    else:
        body = second.json()
        assert "cooldown" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
               "pending" in body.get("message", "").lower()

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: No valid authentication token is provided (missing or expired)
Account ID exists in the system
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with valid body {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 1000} but NO Authorization header
Assert no limit record is created
expected: Response body contains error indicating authentication failure
No pending limit record is created for the account
trace: R-FIN-067: Negative paths — unauthorised must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    api_client, test_account_id
):
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "online", "daily_limit": 1000},
        headers={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") in (
        "UNAUTHORIZED", "AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED", "INVALID_TOKEN"
    ), f"Unexpected error_code: {body.get('error_code')}"
